deltas.hypothesis: Adding one numeric constant, one numeric indicator, and one aligned wire introduces a single auto-chain compressed-wire chunk with no trailing geometry.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42.vi is the baseline for the 0208 straight-wire sentinel used by ChainAutoPath.

deltas.hypothesis: Changing an integer numeric constant to floating-point changes VCTP/type metadata while preserving the simple auto-chain topology.
deltas.evidence: Both fixtures remain simple single-wire networks; descriptor-level interpretation still needs VCTP Inner decoding.

deltas.hypothesis: Boolean terminals use the same straight auto-chain wire family as numeric terminals, while widget class mapping changes from numeric to boolean.
deltas.evidence: BoolToLED.vi provides a non-numeric straight-wire control/indicator pair.

deltas.hypothesis: Moving the sink horizontally changes terminal geometry but does not require a new source-side chain payload value; the renderer stretches the post-elbow segment from terminal bounds.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42Far.vi is used as the far-spacing control for ChainAutoPath rendering.

deltas.hypothesis: Moving the sink 8 px down changes the auto-chain y-step magnitude to 8 while preserving source anchor semantics.
deltas.evidence: Wire tests compare the decoded ChainAutoPath y-step against the controlled 8 px fixture.

deltas.hypothesis: Increasing the vertical displacement from 8 px to 16 px doubles the y-step magnitude.
deltas.evidence: The pair separates magnitude from sign and horizontal routing effects.

deltas.hypothesis: Moving the sink above the source changes the direction flag while preserving y-step magnitude.
deltas.evidence: The up/down fixture pair ground-truths ChainAutoPath sign handling.

deltas.hypothesis: Two disconnected const-to-indicator pairs emit two independent compressed-wire chunks.
deltas.evidence: CountWireMix observes two auto-chain networks in the two-network fixture.

deltas.hypothesis: Moving each branch endpoint perturbs the corresponding trailing endpoint record while earlier structural records remain topology data.
deltas.evidence: TreeEndpointPair is grounded by this geometry-varied pair.

deltas.hypothesis: For pure 3-branch Y trees, the last waypoint-count-minus-4 records are endpoint coordinates.
deltas.evidence: Moving the bottom branch 8 px changes one trailing endpoint record and matches the independent reference-find-by-id.vi shape.

deltas.hypothesis: Comb payloads carry junction/span records around the moved branch rather than a simple trailing endpoint list.
deltas.evidence: The middle-branch move changes adjacent records in opposite directions, so byte0 >= 8 shapes stay raw until more comb variations are authored.

deltas.hypothesis: Four-output networks produce larger topology-dependent tree payloads that need additional fixture variation before endpoint extraction is safe.
deltas.evidence: The larger tree fixture is intentionally not decoded by TreeEndpoints.

deltas.hypothesis: The pure Y-tree endpoint rule observed in controlled fixtures also appears in at least one independent real corpus VI.
deltas.evidence: reference-find-by-id.vi carries a matching shape used as an independent sanity check.

deltas.hypothesis: Forcing LabVIEW's auto-router to take a Z-shape by placing the indicator left of the source produces a 6-waypoint chain payload (06 08 00 01 00 00 10 0f 89 30) instead of the 4-waypoint single-elbow form (04 08 00 00 41 08). Provides ground truth for the multi-elbow ChainAutoPath gap (>4 varints currently rejected by the decoder).
deltas.evidence: Both fixtures share heap-object IDs (95, 51 in termList) and SDF/SimTun anchors; only the wire chunk payload differs.

deltas.hypothesis: Two right-to-left auto-routes with different terminal offsets produce 6-waypoint chains that differ only in the trailing magnitude varints (06 08 00 01 00 00 10 0f 89 30 vs 06 08 00 00 00 00 10 0f 34 23). Isolates per-elbow geometry from direction sign in the multi-elbow encoding.
deltas.evidence: Heap-object IDs identical to the baseline _indicator_left.vi fixture; wire chunk content is the only delta.

deltas.hypothesis: Manual elbow edits made through the LabVIEW editor's normal drag handles get cleaned up by the auto-router when the resulting path is still expressible as a single L-shape; the saved payload retains byte1=0x08 (auto) rather than promoting to byte1=0x04 (manual). Confirms Numeric42Bend.vi remains the only manual-chain ground truth in the corpus.
deltas.evidence: Saved content is 04 08 00 00 fe 70 — same 4-waypoint single-elbow shape as Numeric42_8px_down.vi (04 08 00 00 41 08); only the y-step magnitude varint differs.
